title: Canada Announces Tariff Remission Process for Canadian Businesses Importing Certain Chinese Goods
content: Canada has implemented tariffs on certain Chinese imports, including a 100% surtax on electric vehicles (EVs) and a 25% surtax on steel and aluminum, to protect its industries from unfair competition. The federal government has launched a process for businesses to request remission of these surtaxes under specific circumstances, such as supply chain challenges or existing contracts. Remission will provide relief or refunds for affected businesses, and the process may also apply retroactively or to future surtaxes on critical manufacturing products like batteries and semiconductors.
author: Analysts Account
